steps.title: Check if the equations are equivalent
steps.content: The second equation is $24x + 21y = 27$. Divide by 3: $$\frac{24x + 21y}{3} = \frac{27}{3}$$ This simplifies to $8x + 7y = 9$, which is exactly the first equation. Both equations represent the same line—there are infinitely many solutions.

steps.title: Solve for the parametric form of points on the line
steps.content: From $8x + 7y = 9$, solve for $y$: $$7y = -8x + 9$$ $$y = -\frac{8x}{7} + \frac{9}{7}$$ Using $r$ as the parameter (so $x = r$), points on the line are $(r, -\frac{8r}{7} + \frac{9}{7})$ for each real number $r$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
